title: Purification of in vitro transcribed RNA with the Zymo Research RNA Clean & Concentrator™-25 Kit
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"82bda","text":"This is a protocol for purification of in vitro transcribed RNA via the Zymo Research RNA Clean \u0026 Concentrator™-25 kit.  It works with capped and uncapped mRNA (usually \u003e600 nt) with or without poly(A)-tails , shRNA (49 nt) and single guide sgRNA (100 nt) and yields RNA of high purity and concentration. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"baifl","text":"The maximum yield for RNA is given as 50 µg but for RNAs shorter than 200 nt, we found it to work to up to 200 µg.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fs0ml","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4kbjo","text":"The optional DNase I digestion step is only necessary if digestion of DNA was not part of the in vitro transcription.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":94,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"f4h6s","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ma1n","text":"For larger amounts of input RNA (\u003e200 µg shRNA or sgRNA or \u003e50 µg mRNA) use Zymo Research Quick-RNA™ Miniprep Kit and follow instructions for reaction clean-up; elute in ≥50 µl 95°C-hot water and re-apply eluate once. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"eku26","text":"Heat nuclease-free water to 95°C for elution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"btq76","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"af1nm","text":"Label one set of Zymo-Spin IICR columns (clear) and two sets of RNase-free 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tubes with the sample names.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e9c75","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7e0p3","text":"Preparation of reagents before first usage:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7q4t2","text":"Prepare RNA Wash Buffer:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fb4ri","text":"  50-purifications kit (R1017): add 48 ml 100% ethanol to the 12 ml RNA Wash Buffer concentrate","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3s52f","text":"  100-purifications kit (R1018): add 96 ml 100% ethanol to the 24 ml RNA Wash Buffer concentrate","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1klse","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b07ub","text":"OPTIONAL: Reconstitute DNase I (250 U) with 275 µL nuclease-free water and store 50-µL aliquots at −20°C.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bh6cp","text":"Use only RNase-free reagents and consumables and follow general rules for the work with RNA such as use of regularly changed gloves, treatment of any surfaces touched during the extraction with RNase decontamination solution (e.g., RNase Away or RNaseZAP) and keeping the work area clean and tidy. NEB has a nice guide here: https://www.neb.com/en-us/tools-and-resources/usage-guidelines/avoiding-ribonuclease-contamination","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":325,"length":98}],"data":{}},{"key":"722bv","text":"Once RNA is eluted into nuclease-free water, keep it on ice and finish quality control, quantification and make aliquots in one go to store the aliquots at −80°C. Having the RNA on ice longer than necessary increases the risk of degradation. Degradation will also occur from freeze-thaw cycles (hence the single-use aliquots).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"2C08B643423211F0AB870A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.neb.com/en-us/tools-and-resources/usage-guidelines/avoiding-ribonuclease-contamination"}}}}

For in-column DNA digestion:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":38}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fjti0","text":"Zymo Research DNase I Set (250 U) - E1010","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"881a8","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"15e0t","text":"Reagents to have ready for purification:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":40}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"aus5a","text":"­100% ethanol, molecular biology grade, ~150 µL per 50 µL initial sample","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"k59f","text":"Nuclease-free water (supplied in kit but better use 2-mL aliquots from IDT: 11-04-02-01), ~25 µL/sample, more for final concentration adjustment after purification","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"96FD70074AFF11F08D8D0A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.zymoresearch.com/products/rna-clean-concentrator-25"}}}}
steps: Add an equal volume of 100% ethanol and mix well. E.g. , for 50 µL initial sample plus 100 µL RNA Binding buffer, add 150 μl ethanol.
Add 1.5 µL nuclease-free water into a RNase-free PCR tube.
Add 1.5 µL of the purified RNA to the water, mix by pipetting and put on ice.
Re-apply the eluate one more time to the column matrix and centrifuge centrifuge at 16,000 ×g for 30 s at RT. This increases the recovery (especially when using less than 50 µl volume).
Add 25 µL of 95°C-hot nuclease-free water directly to the column matrix and centrifuge centrifuge at 16,000 ×g for 30 s at RT.

Add 400 µL RNA Wash buffer to the column and centrifuge centrifuge at 16,000 ×g for 1 min at RT toensure complete removal of the wash buffer. Transfer the column carefully into the first labelled RNase-free 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube.
Add 400 µL RNA Prep buffer to the column and centrifuge centrifuge at 16,000 ×g for 30 s at RT. Discard the flow-through.

Transfer the sample to the Zymo-Spin IICR Column (clear) in a 2-mL Collection tube and centrifuge at 16,000 ×g for 30 s at RT. Discard the flow-through. Older versions of the kit had the Zymo-Spin™ IIC columns supplied and work as well, the only difference is an added retention ring for the matrix.
Add 2 volumes of RNA Binding Buffer to the sample and mix well. E.g. , for 50 μl sample, add 100 µL RNA Binding buffer.

Transfer the RNA into the second labelled RNase-free tube and keep on ice while preparing the quantification and quality control steps. The new tube just prevents contaminantion of the RNA; the open lid was exposed to the centrifuge and probably touched during the elution steps.
Transfer 1 µL of the mix into a new PCR tube and prepare for electrophoresis, keep on ice. This is to check on RNA integrity and size.

Use the prepared sample to check RNA size and integrity by gel electrophoresis. Depending on the RNA size, agarose gels of 1% (mRNA) to 2% (shorter RNA) provide sufficient resolution. We usually use one of the two electrophoresis protocols: 1: TAE+bleach agarose gels and standard DNA ladders and loading dyes (to see integrity and get a rough estimate of size): RNA agarose electrophoresis with bleach gels 2: Standard TAE agarose gels but in combination with denaturing RNA loading dye and an RNA ladder for a better size assessment (e.g., when a new construct has been transcribed the first time): RNA agarose electrophoresis with denaturing RNA sample buffer
Add 400 µL RNA Wash buffer to the column and centrifuge centrifuge at 16,000 ×g for 30 s at RT. Discard the flow through.
Make a DNase I reaction mix in an RNase-free 1.5-mL tube: per sample add 5 µL DNase I to 75 µL DNA Digestion buffer. Both reagents are part of the DNase I set which must be ordered separately.
Add 80 µLof the DNase I reaction mix to the column matrix and incubate at RT for 15 min.
Add 700 µL RNA Wash buffer to the column and centrifuge centrifuge at 16,000 ×g for 30 s at RT. Discard the flow-through.
Store aliquots at −80°C.
Dilute the original purified RNA to the desired concentration and make single-use aliquots in pre-cooled, labelled RNase-free 1.5 mL tubes on ice.

Calculate the RNA concentration (double the measured value to account for the 1:2 dilution in step 14). The A 260 /A 280 ratio should be ≥2.0, A 260 /A 230 ≥2.2 for a good quality RNA purification (in regards of contaminants).
From the remaining mix, use 1 µL to quantify the RNA on a NanoDrop device blanked with nuclease-free water.
creator: Christian CR Renicke
